executiveSummary.criticalFinding: The restaurant is leaking bookings during high-intent calls due to availability rigidity and booking friction. At least 21 booking attempts failed, with multiple callers declining alternatives when their preferred slot was unavailable.
executiveSummary.revenueImpact: Estimated 105 covers lost during the period. At £55 average cover value this represents roughly £5,775 in unrealised revenue.
executiveSummary.immediateAction: Introduce automated alternative slot suggestions and waitlist capture for fully booked periods. The bot should propose nearby times immediately rather than ending the call after one unavailable slot.

revenueInsights.headline: Peak lunch slots are turning away demand

signal.description: Multiple callers requested lunch around 12:30–13:30 and were rejected.
examples: Call 16: lunch for four at 1 PM declined after alternative offered at 2:15 PM. Call 18: lunch request at 12:45 declined when unavailable.

impact.description: Lost midday covers where demand already exists.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 15, 16 and 18 show callers requesting lunch around 12:45–1:00 but declining later times such as 2:15 PM. When alternatives are too far away, guests simply abandon.

action.description: Introduce tighter slot increments (e.g., 15-minute seating) and offer earlier/later nearby options immediately.

quotes: Call 16: lunch for four at 1 PM declined after alternative offered at 2:15 PM. Call 18: lunch request at 12:45 declined when unavailable.

revenueInsights.headline: Large group demand signal being under-captured

signal.description: Several large-party requests required manual follow-up rather than immediate booking.
examples: Call 55: request for 80-person booking sent to front office via email rather than secured.

impact.description: Large parties represent £400–£4,000 revenue per booking.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 55 (80 guests), 105 (65 guests Christmas event) and 163 (9 guests) required escalation to the team rather than instant booking.

action.description: Create a structured large-party capture flow that collects date, guest count and contact details then schedules an events callback automatically.

quotes: Call 55: request for 80-person booking sent to front office via email rather than secured.

revenueInsights.headline: Mother’s Day demand spike emerging two weeks early

signal.description: Multiple enquiries about Mother’s Day bookings.
examples: Call 147: booking for 11 guests breakfast. Call 148: Mother’s Day booking enquiry requiring deposit.

impact.description: High-value service day with premium spend potential.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 9, 74, 124, 131, 144, 147, 148 and 158 all reference Mother’s Day reservations.

action.description: Pre-sell remaining seats via outbound SMS or email campaigns and enforce deposits to secure covers.

quotes: Call 147: booking for 11 guests breakfast. Call 148: Mother’s Day booking enquiry requiring deposit.

revenueInsights.headline: Operational questions indicate unclear website information

signal.description: Repeated calls asking about lift availability, directions, and menus.
examples: Call 171: guest asked whether the lift would be working during a May visit.

impact.description: Front-of-house time consumed by basic questions instead of bookings.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 19, 106, 154, 171 ask about lift accessibility. Calls 46 and 48 request directions.

action.description: Add accessibility status and directions prominently to website and booking confirmation emails.

quotes: Call 171: guest asked whether the lift would be working during a May visit.

revenueInsights.headline: Customers asking for services the restaurant does not offer

signal.description: Multiple callers asking about afternoon tea.
examples: Call 20: caller asked about afternoon tea and ended call after learning it was unavailable.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 20, 49 and 164 show guests asking for afternoon tea but being told it is not available.

action.description: Either introduce limited afternoon tea or add a scripted upsell for lunch bookings instead.

quotes: Call 20: caller asked about afternoon tea and ended call after learning it was unavailable.

revenueInsights.headline: Special occasions are frequent booking drivers

signal.description: Multiple bookings mention birthdays, anniversaries and dates.
examples: Call 37 included Prosecco request for first date.

impact.description: These bookings typically generate higher spend.

action.description: Train the bot to upsell wine, desserts or celebration packages when special occasions are detected.

quotes: Call 37 included Prosecco request for first date.

revenueInsights.headline: Booking abandonment during data collection

signal.description: Several callers hang up when asked for contact details.
examples: Call 97 ended after the agent asked for email confirmation.

revenueInsights.reasoning: Calls 96, 97, 101, 102 and 103 show callers disconnecting during name or email capture.

action.description: Reduce required fields and confirm booking before collecting optional details.

quotes: Call 97 ended after the agent asked for email confirmation.

botPerformanceIssues.title: Booking drop-off during contact detail capture

problem.description: Users frequently hang up when the bot asks for full contact details before confirming the booking.

callerImpact.description: Guests abandon the process despite confirmed availability.

trainingRecommendation.description: Confirm reservation first, then collect optional details after.

problem.description: When requested slots are unavailable, alternatives offered are hours apart.

callerImpact.description: Customers decline options and abandon booking.

trainingRecommendation.description: Offer nearest two slots within ±30 minutes automatically.

botPerformanceIssues.title: High transfer dependency for operational queries

problem.description: The bot transfers calls for lost items, vouchers and staff requests.

callerImpact.description: Longer resolution times and additional workload on staff.

trainingRecommendation.description: Add structured flows for lost property, vouchers and deposit payments.

botPerformanceIssues.title: Guests questioning if the bot is real

problem.description: Some callers suspect they are speaking to an automated system.

callerImpact.description: Reduced trust and potential early call termination.

trainingRecommendation.description: Improve conversational fillers and natural acknowledgement phrases.

botPerformanceIssues.title: Limited ability to modify bookings directly

problem.description: Several booking changes require manual follow-up via email.

callerImpact.description: Guests leave without confirmation of requested changes.

trainingRecommendation.description: Enable live modification of bookings within reservation system.

opportunity.description: Capture bookings that currently drop when slots are unavailable.

immediate: Offer waitlist immediately and send SMS confirmation with available times if cancellations occur.

opportunity.description: Special occasions appear in many bookings and can increase average spend.

immediate: Offer wine, cake or celebration packages when the bot detects birthdays or anniversaries.

strategicRecommendations.title: Launch pre-selling campaign for Mother’s Day

opportunity.description: High booking interest already visible in call data.

immediate: Email and SMS campaigns promoting limited remaining tables with deposit requirement.

strategicRecommendations.successMetric: 100% seat occupancy for Mother's Day services.

immediate: Add clear pages for accessibility, directions, menus and service types.

topQuestions.question: Do you offer Jain or vegetarian food?

missingFields: party size missing in some booking calls
exact booking confirmation status unclear in several summaries
